name: Kahlua Stir-Fry Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Chicken, boneless, skinless, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a shallow bowl, mix the beaten egg, 2 tablespoons oil, and cornstarch to coat the chicken.
2. Heat the remaining oil in a wok over medium-high heat.
3. Add the coated chicken to the hot oil and stir-fry until golden brown. Remove the chicken to a plate or bowl and let it drain well, leaving very little oil in the wok.
4. Add the waterchestnuts, mushrooms, bamboo shoots, bean sprouts, and snow peas (if using) to the wok. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
5. Mix 1 tablespoon of cornstarch with 3 tablespoons Kahlua in a small bowl.
6. Add the Kahlua-cornstarch mixture to the wok and stir well.
7. Return the chicken to the wok, stirring to coat evenly.
8. Stir in the cashews and mix well.
9. Transfer the dish to a heated serving platter.
10. Makes 4-6 servings.
